meta.title: Careers | Al-Hayaat School
meta.description: Join the Al-Hayaat School team. Explore career opportunities and help shape the next generation of leaders.

hero.headline: Ready to make an Impact? Join us on Our Journey.

intro.heading: Welcome to Al-Hayaat School
intro.body:
At Al-Hayaat School, our mission is to provide a well-rounded education rooted in the teachings of the Quran and Ahlulbayt [A.S.]. Guided by the principles of حَيَاةٌ طَيِّبَةٌ (pure life), we are dedicated to cultivating a conducive learning environment that nurtures values, academic excellence, and holistic development. Our school is set to open in 2026, and we are actively building a team of passionate educators and professionals to help bring our vision to life.

This is more than a job—it's an opportunity to shape the next generation of leaders, innovators, and contributors to humanity and the community.

reasons.title: Faith-Driven Mission
reasons.description: Contribute to a school grounded in Quranic teachings and the values of Ahlulbayt [A.S.]

reasons.title: Innovative Education
reasons.description: Be part of an institution that integrates morals with high academic standards.

reasons.title: Collaborative Growth
reasons.description: Work alongside a dedicated team, benefiting from tailored professional development and training programs.

reasons.title: Community Impact
reasons.description: Help establish a much-needed school in the Kitchener-Waterloo region, addressing the educational needs and aspirations of local families.

positions.title: Elementary School Teacher
positions.description: As an elementary teacher, you will provide a well-rounded education rooted in both academic excellence and values. This role requires delivering core subjects (such as Language Arts, Mathematics, Science, and Social Studies), all while fostering a nurturing and respectful classroom environment that aligns with principles.
responsibilities: Integrate principles into lessons and school activities to support the students' understanding of their faith.
Collaborate with the school administration to ensure the curriculum includes aspects of Studies and the Quran.
Teach core subjects while incorporating values, fostering a respectful, disciplined, and inclusive classroom.
Use varied teaching methods to engage different learning styles and support students' academic and social growth.
Manage classroom behavior by setting high standards in alignment with the school's values.
Serve as a role model for students, embodying the principles of conduct and ethics.
Provide moral and character education, guiding students in kindness, respect, and personal responsibility.
Regularly assess and monitor student progress, including through formative and summative assessments.
Provide detailed reports and constructive feedback to students and parents, fostering open communication with families.
Participate in ongoing professional development to stay current with teaching approaches.
Work collaboratively with other teachers and administrative staff to create a supportive educational environment.
Respond to various inquiries from students' parents/guardians regarding instructional programs and student progress.
Engage with the broader school community by leading or participating in school events, assemblies, and other activities.
Plan and supervise extra-curricular activities, field trips, or events that enrich students' learning and experiences.
Attend staff meetings and prepare and deliver Parent-Teacher meetings.
qualifications: Degree or diploma in Education or a relevant field. An Ontario Teaching Certification (OCT) is often preferred.
Prior teaching experience in an elementary school setting; experience in an environment is an asset.
Familiarity with the teachings of Ahlul-Bayt to align with the school's mission.
skills: Strong knowledge of Ontario's elementary curriculum and teaching standards.
Understanding teachings, culture, and values, with the ability to incorporate them appropriately into the curriculum.
Proficiency in English is a must.
Proficiency in Arabic is often preferred but not always required.
positions.application_note: We encourage all interested candidates to apply, even if you don't meet every requirement listed. Skills and experience can be developed; we value potential and a willingness to learn. If this role excites you and you believe you can bring value to our team, we want to hear from you!
positions.how_to_apply: Send an email to admin@alhayaat.ca that includes your cover letter and resume.

cta.heading: Support Our Mission
cta.body: Together, we're building a brighter future for our children and the community.
